title: haplophyllum elatum
intro: Haplophyllum elatum, also known as the 'Rock Spurge', is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, derived from its sap and leaves. The Rock Spurge has been reported to have various health benefits, such as aiding in wound healing, reducing fever, and treating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
